refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR conflates three distinct obligations: (1) deriving requirements from mission context, (2) recording them, and (3) managing them over time. Atomicity requires separating these concerns. CR-NGHE-005.1 focuses on the derivation activity—the logical transformation from mission inputs to performance targets—which is solution-neutral and testable. Recording and management are separate concerns addressed in sub-FRs.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Provide a worksite mission scenario (e.g., excavation productivity target, safety constraints, energy budget) and verify that the system produces a documented set of performance targets (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) that are traceable to the input mission parameters.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The NGHE solution shall derive performance requirements from worksite mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.2: The NGHE solution shall record derived performance requirements with traceability to worksite mission inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The NGHE solution shall maintain a history of performance requirement baselines and calibration updates.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The NGHE solution shall manage performance requirement changes in accordance with worksite operational policies.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Establish a parameterized mission-context intake process that captures worksite operational profile (hazard class, duty cycle, environmental conditions, safety constraints) and maps each input to a corresponding performance target via a documented traceability matrix, with version control for baseline snapshots.

DP-B: Implement a rule-based inference engine that translates worksite safety plan directives and operational policies into quantified performance constraints, storing results in a structured requirement specification with audit trail.

DP-C: Design a two-stage derivation workflow: (1) extract mission context from worksite documentation (safety plan, operational procedures, hazard assessments); (2) apply a standardized performance-mapping template to convert each context element into measurable targets, with manual review gates and sign-off checkpoints.

DP-D: Create a federated data model where worksite mission context is stored as immutable baseline records, linked to derived performance targets via explicit traceability relations, enabling historical comparison and change impact analysis without re-derivation.

DP-E: Establish a hybrid human-in-the-loop process: automated parsing of worksite inputs feeds a decision support tool that recommends performance targets; domain experts validate and approve, with all decisions logged in an auditable registry.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-C (two-stage derivation workflow with manual review gates) combined with DP-D (federated data model for traceability and history)

potential_sources: DP-C and DP-D are loosely coupled: DP-C defines the logical process (extraction + mapping + review), while DP-D specifies the data architecture (immutable baselines + traceability relations). They can be implemented independently; DP-D can support any derivation process.
Risk of tight coupling if DP-C's review gates are hardcoded into DP-D's data model. Mitigation: keep review metadata separate from baseline/target records.
Potential coupling with external worksite documentation systems (safety plan, operational procedures). Mitigation: define a clear input interface specification independent of source system.
coupling_check.notes: The combination avoids monolithic inference engines (DP-B) that embed domain logic, and avoids over-automation (DP-E) that may obscure traceability. DP-C + DP-D preserves human accountability while enabling systematic history tracking.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite safety plan and operational policies are available in machine-readable or structured format (or can be reliably extracted). RISK: If inputs are unstructured (free-text documents), extraction fidelity may be low. MITIGATION: Define a worksite input template/checklist to standardize capture.

ASSUMPTION: Performance targets can be deterministically derived from mission context via a repeatable mapping template. RISK: Some targets may require domain expertise or iterative refinement not captured in a static template. MITIGATION: Include expert review gates (DP-C) and allow template evolution via change control.

ASSUMPTION: Baseline snapshots and calibration updates are infrequent enough to manage manually. RISK: If worksite conditions change frequently, manual versioning becomes a bottleneck. MITIGATION: Define trigger criteria for baseline updates (e.g., safety plan revision, hazard reassessment) and automate version creation on trigger.

ASSUMPTION: Traceability matrix can be maintained without significant overhead. RISK: If performance targets are numerous or interdependent, traceability becomes complex. MITIGATION: Use DP-D's federated model to store relations as first-class entities, not spreadsheet rows.

ASSUMPTION: Acceptance criteria (documented set, traceability, history) can be satisfied without real-time derivation. RISK: If worksite context changes mid-operation, derived targets may become stale. MITIGATION: Define refresh cadence and change notification protocol.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine FR-NGHE-005.1 to explicitly state: 'The NGHE solution shall provide a two-stage process to derive performance requirements: (1) structured intake of worksite mission context (safety plan, operational policies, hazard profile, duty cycle); (2) deterministic mapping of context elements to quantified performance targets via a documented template, with expert review and approval gates. All derivations shall be recorded in an immutable baseline registry with full traceability to source inputs and a complete history of baseline versions and calibration updates.' This revision clarifies the logical boundary (DP-C) and data architecture (DP-D) expected, reducing ambiguity in IM design.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Centralized orchestration layer that implements the two-stage derivation workflow (DP-C) with manual review gates and maintains the federated data model (DP-D) for traceability and history across all operational domains

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives task requests and derivation inputs that trigger the two-stage workflow; stage 1 (automated derivation) processes incoming tasking

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Sends safety-critical review gates and alerts during stage 2 (manual review); receives safety constraint feedback to inform derivation decisions

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Exports traceability records, derivation history, and audit logs from the federated data model to external monitoring and compliance systems

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Distributes derivation results and federated state updates to Vehicle and Teleoperation; receives acknowledgments and status updates for history tracking

module_containment_scores.notes: Excellent fit: NGHE Solution is the logical orchestration hub for multi-stage workflows and centralized data governance. The two-stage derivation (DP-C) naturally belongs in a solution-level coordinator. The federated data model (DP-D) is best maintained at the solution level to ensure consistent traceability across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Minor diffusion: stage 2 manual review gates require SafetyAlert interface coupling, but this is expected and well-defined.

Poor fit: Vehicle is a physical/operational domain, not a coordination layer. Placing the two-stage workflow and federated data model here would scatter orchestration logic across the vehicle platform and create tight coupling to vehicle-specific implementations. The workflow must remain agnostic to vehicle type. High diffusion: traceability and history would need to propagate back to other domains (Teleoperation, Safety Services), violating cohesion.

Poor fit: Teleoperation is a specific operational mode, not a system-wide orchestrator. The two-stage derivation workflow and federated data model must serve all operational contexts (autonomous, teleoperated, mixed). Placing them in Teleoperation would create mode-specific logic that should be mode-agnostic. High diffusion: Vehicle and Safety Services would need to query or replicate the data model, creating inconsistency.

Moderate-poor fit: Safety Services is a functional domain focused on safety constraints and alerts, not workflow orchestration or data governance. While stage 2 manual review gates are safety-critical, the entire two-stage workflow and federated data model should not be owned by Safety Services. This would conflate safety validation with derivation orchestration. Moderate diffusion: the workflow and data model would need to be accessible to Vehicle and Teleoperation, creating cross-domain dependencies.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module provides the optimal boundary for containing both DP-C (two-stage derivation workflow with manual review gates) and DP-D (federated data model for traceability and history). As the system-level orchestrator, it is responsible for coordinating multi-stage decision processes and maintaining authoritative state across all operational domains. The workflow is inherently solution-level (not vehicle-specific, mode-specific, or safety-specific), and the federated data model must be centralized to ensure consistency and auditability. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) provide clear boundaries for input, validation, output, and distribution without requiring the DP logic to diffuse into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If the NGHE Solution module becomes a monolithic orchestrator, the two-stage workflow and data model may become difficult to test and evolve independently. Mitigation: decompose the workflow and data model into internal sub-components (e.g., Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) with clear internal interfaces.

Risk: The federated data model may grow to include domain-specific attributes that blur the boundary between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services. Mitigation: enforce a strict schema contract; domain-specific extensions should be stored as opaque metadata or in domain-owned stores, not in the core federated model.

Risk: Manual review gates (stage 2) may require tight coupling to Safety Services for real-time constraint checking. If Safety Services evolves independently, the workflow may become brittle. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert as an asynchronous, event-driven interface with clear request/response contracts and timeout handling.

Risk: Telemetry export of traceability records may create a large surface area for external systems to depend on the data model schema. Mitigation: version the Telemetry interface and provide a stable, minimal export schema that decouples external consumers from internal data model evolution.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: No revision to DP choice is necessary. DP-C and DP-D are well-suited to the NGHE Solution boundary. However, to further improve containment and reduce diffusion risk, consider refining the DP specification to explicitly separate concerns: (1) DP-C.1: Orchestrate two-stage derivation workflow with pluggable review gate logic (owned by NGHE Solution), and (2) DP-C.2: Implement stage 2 manual review gate evaluation logic (owned by Safety Services, invoked via SafetyAlert interface). This keeps orchestration centralized while allowing Safety Services to own safety-specific validation rules. Similarly, for DP-D, clarify that the federated data model stores only cross-domain traceability and history; domain-specific operational state remains in Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. This refinement maintains the DP intent while improving module cohesion and reducing coupling.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-C (two-stage derivation workflow with manual review gates) and DP-D (federated data model for traceability and history) directly address FR-NGHE-005.1's requirement to derive performance requirements from worksite mission context. DP-C provides the mechanism for systematic derivation with safety validation; DP-D ensures traceability and historical audit trail. Both DPs are logically sound and necessary to satisfy the FR. No logical gaps or contradictions detected.

diffusion_risks: MODERATE: Stage 2 manual review gates (DP-C) couple tightly to SafetyAlert interface. If Safety Services evolves its constraint model independently, the workflow may require rework. Mitigation: define SafetyAlert as an asynchronous, event-driven contract with explicit timeout and fallback behavior.

MODERATE: Federated data model (DP-D) may accumulate domain-specific attributes over time, blurring the boundary between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services. Mitigation: enforce strict schema governance; domain-specific extensions must be stored as opaque metadata or in domain-owned stores, not in the core federated model.

LOW: Telemetry export of traceability records creates external dependency surface. If the data model schema evolves, external consumers (compliance systems, monitoring) may break. Mitigation: version the Telemetry interface and provide a stable, minimal export schema decoupled from internal model evolution.

LOW: NGHE Solution module may become monolithic if the two-stage workflow and data model are not internally decomposed. Mitigation: decompose into internal sub-components (Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) with clear internal interfaces to enable independent testing and evolution.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 0.92 containment score with 0.08 diffusion score. This is excellent. The module is the logical orchestration hub for multi-stage workflows and centralized data governance. DP-C (two-stage derivation) is inherently solution-level, not vehicle-specific, mode-specific, or safety-specific. DP-D (federated data model) must be centralized to ensure consistency and auditability across all operational domains. External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) provide clear boundaries without requiring DP logic to diffuse into Vehicle, Teleoperation, or Safety Services. Alternative placements (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services) all score poorly (0.35–0.42 containment, 0.58–0.65 diffusion) due to domain-specific scope and lack of orchestration authority.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: HIGH. The NGHE Solution module boundary enables direct verification of FR-NGHE-005.1 acceptance criteria: (1) 'Documented set of performance targets traceable to worksite inputs' is verifiable via DP-D's federated data model, which maintains traceability records and derivation history; (2) 'History of baselines/calibration updates' is verifiable via DP-D's audit logs and version tracking. The two-stage workflow (DP-C) with manual review gates provides a documented derivation process. Test cases can be constructed to inject worksite mission context (via Tasking interface), verify stage 1 automated derivation, validate stage 2 manual review gates (via SafetyAlert), and confirm traceability records are persisted and exported (via Telemetry). No verification gaps identified.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: HIGH. Ownership is clearly allocated to NGHE Solution (Architecture/Orchestration Team). Responsibilities are well-defined: (1) DP-C orchestration: NGHE Solution owns the two-stage workflow and review gate sequencing; Safety Services owns the safety constraint evaluation logic (invoked via SafetyAlert). (2) DP-D governance: NGHE Solution owns the federated data model schema and traceability store; Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services own domain-specific operational state (not in the federated model). External interfaces (Tasking, SafetyAlert, Telemetry, Network Link) establish clear input/output/feedback boundaries. No ambiguity in module ownership or responsibility allocation.

recommended_revisions: REFINE DP-C: Decompose into DP-C.1 (Orchestrate two-stage derivation workflow with pluggable review gate logic, owned by NGHE Solution) and DP-C.2 (Implement stage 2 manual review gate evaluation logic, owned by Safety Services, invoked via SafetyAlert interface). This keeps orchestration centralized while allowing Safety Services to own safety-specific validation rules, improving cohesion and reducing coupling.

REFINE DP-D: Clarify that the federated data model stores only cross-domain traceability, derivation history, and audit logs. Domain-specific operational state (e.g., vehicle telemetry, teleoperation mode state, safety constraint details) remains owned by Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules. This prevents schema bloat and maintains clear module boundaries.

FORMALIZE SafetyAlert Interface: Define SafetyAlert as an asynchronous, event-driven contract with explicit request/response schemas, timeout handling, and fallback behavior. This reduces coupling between NGHE Solution and Safety Services and enables independent evolution.

VERSION Telemetry Export: Establish a versioned, stable export schema for Telemetry that decouples external consumers (compliance systems, monitoring) from internal federated data model evolution. Document the schema contract and deprecation policy.

DECOMPOSE NGHE Solution Internally: Create internal sub-components (Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) with clear internal interfaces to enable independent testing, evolution, and potential future distribution across multiple physical/logical nodes if scalability demands arise.

decision.justification: All three gates (FR-DP, DP-IM, FR-IM) pass with high confidence. However, the recommended_revisions identify critical decomposition opportunities that must be resolved before proceeding to detailed design. Specifically: (1) DP-C requires decomposition into orchestration logic (NGHE Solution) and safety-specific review gate evaluation (Safety Services) to reduce coupling and improve cohesion; (2) DP-D requires clarification of federated model scope to prevent schema bloat and maintain module boundaries; (3) SafetyAlert interface must be formalized as an asynchronous contract to enable independent evolution; (4) NGHE Solution itself requires internal decomposition into Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, and TraceabilityStore sub-components to prevent monolithic growth and enable testability. Given high safety criticality, these architectural refinements are essential before deriving sub-FRs. A ZIGZAG_DOWN approach (revise current level, then decompose) is more prudent than direct STOP or immediate sub-FR derivation.
next_level_sub_frs: FR-NGHE-005.1.1: Orchestrate two-stage performance requirement derivation workflow with pluggable review gate logic, ensuring stage 1 automated derivation executes before stage 2 manual review gates, and all derivation decisions are logged for audit.

FR-NGHE-005.1.2: Implement stage 2 manual review gate evaluation logic that validates derived performance requirements against safety constraints, operational feasibility, and mission context, with explicit pass/fail/rework decision points.

FR-NGHE-005.1.3: Maintain federated traceability data model that records derivation history, baseline versions, calibration updates, and audit logs, ensuring cross-domain consistency without accumulating domain-specific operational state.

FR-NGHE-005.1.4: Formalize SafetyAlert interface as an asynchronous, event-driven contract with explicit request/response schemas, timeout handling, and fallback behavior to enable independent evolution of Safety Services.

FR-NGHE-005.1.5: Decompose NGHE Solution into internal sub-components (Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) with clear internal interfaces to enable independent testing, evolution, and potential future distribution.
FR-NGHE-005.1.6: Version and stabilize Telemetry export schema for traceability records, decoupling external consumers (compliance systems, monitoring) from internal federated data model evolution.

revise.why: DP-C and DP-D require refinement before sub-FR derivation. DP-C must be explicitly decomposed into DP-C.1 (Orchestrate two-stage workflow, owned by NGHE Solution) and DP-C.2 (Implement stage 2 review gate logic, owned by Safety Services, invoked via SafetyAlert). DP-D must clarify that the federated model stores only cross-domain traceability and audit logs, not domain-specific operational state. These refinements reduce coupling, improve cohesion, and establish clear ownership boundaries—critical for high-safety-criticality systems. Additionally, SafetyAlert interface must be formalized as an asynchronous contract with explicit timeout and fallback semantics to mitigate MODERATE diffusion risk. Telemetry export schema must be versioned and decoupled from internal model evolution to mitigate LOW diffusion risk. NGHE Solution must be internally decomposed into Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, and TraceabilityStore sub-components to prevent monolithic growth and enable independent testing.

assumptions: Safety Services module exists and can be invoked asynchronously via SafetyAlert interface with explicit timeout and fallback behavior.
Federated data model can be governed with strict schema controls to prevent domain-specific attribute accumulation.
NGHE Solution can be internally decomposed into three sub-components (Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) without violating orchestration cohesion.
Telemetry export schema can be versioned and decoupled from internal federated model evolution without breaking external consumer contracts.
Two-stage derivation workflow (automated stage 1 + manual review stage 2) is feasible within operational latency constraints for worksite mission context updates.
Manual review gates in stage 2 can be executed by qualified personnel with documented decision rationale and audit trail.
open_risks: MODERATE: SafetyAlert interface coupling. If Safety Services evolves its constraint model independently, the workflow may require rework. Mitigation: formalize SafetyAlert as asynchronous, event-driven contract with explicit timeout and fallback behavior.

MODERATE: Federated data model scope creep. Domain-specific attributes may accumulate over time, blurring boundaries between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Teleoperation/Safety Services. Mitigation: enforce strict schema governance; domain-specific extensions must be stored as opaque metadata or in domain-owned stores.

LOW: Telemetry export external dependency. If data model schema evolves, external consumers (compliance systems, monitoring) may break. Mitigation: version Telemetry interface and provide stable, minimal export schema decoupled from internal model evolution.

LOW: NGHE Solution monolithic growth. If two-stage workflow and data model are not internally decomposed, the module may become difficult to test and evolve independently. Mitigation: decompose into internal sub-components (Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, TraceabilityStore) with clear internal interfaces.

HIGH (Safety-Critical): Manual review gate execution quality. Stage 2 review gates depend on human judgment; inconsistent or incomplete review may miss safety-critical issues. Mitigation: establish clear review criteria, training, and audit procedures; consider semi-automated review support (e.g., constraint violation detection).
evidence_needed: Formal specification of SafetyAlert interface contract (request/response schemas, timeout semantics, fallback behavior).
Schema governance policy for federated data model, including rules for domain-specific extensions and opaque metadata storage.
Versioning and deprecation policy for Telemetry export schema, with examples of backward-compatible evolution.
Internal interface specifications for Stage1Derivation, Stage2ReviewGate, and TraceabilityStore sub-components.
Operational latency analysis: confirm that two-stage derivation workflow (automated + manual review) meets worksite mission context update frequency requirements.
Manual review gate procedure documentation: criteria, decision templates, audit trail format, and training requirements for qualified personnel.
Test cases for two-stage workflow: inject worksite mission context, verify stage 1 automated derivation, validate stage 2 manual review gates, confirm traceability records are persisted and exported.
Verification plan for federated data model: confirm that traceability records are complete, audit logs are immutable, and schema governance is enforced.
Risk assessment for manual review gate execution quality: identify potential failure modes (missed constraints, inconsistent decisions) and mitigation strategies.
